case_id: KOAK Oakland San Francisco Bay Airport

pi_items.source: Oakland International Airport official airport codes (ICAO: KOAK, IATA: OAK). Verified via AirNav, Wikipedia, and multiple aviation database sources.
METAR is the standardized aviation weather report format defined by international aviation authorities. Verified via CFI Notebook, NPS Aviation Weather, and FAA documentation.
KOAK appears at the beginning of every METAR report for Oakland Airport as the airport identifier. This is standard METAR format.

KT is the abbreviation for knots in METAR reports. Wind speed is always reported in knots in METAR format (e.g., 10008KT = wind 100° at 8 knots). Verified via METAR decoding guides and FAA aviation weather documentation.

SM is the abbreviation for statute miles in METAR reports. Visibility in US METAR reports is reported in statute miles (e.g., 10SM = 10 statute miles visibility). Verified via METAR format documentation and aviation weather sources.

privileged_info_summary: KOAK is the correct ICAO code for Oakland International Airport (Oakland San Francisco Bay Airport). The task requires agents to retrieve METAR (Meteorological Aerodrome Report) weather data from the Avare aviation GPS application. A METAR report for KOAK contains three required strings: 'KOAK' (airport identifier), 'KT' (knots, used for wind speed), and 'SM' (statute miles, used for visibility). All weather data comes from real-time FAA aviation weather sources and is not synthetic.
